manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
You are playing a collaborative word guessing game in which you have to describe a target word for another player to guess.

Rules:
(a) You have to reply in the form: CLUE: <some text>. Guesses from the other player will start with GUESS.
(b) You cannot use the target word itself, parts or morphological variants of it in your description.
(c) In addition, the same rules apply for related words which are provided below.

End conditions:
(i) If you use the target word or a related word in your description, then you lose.
(ii) If the other player can guess the target word in 3 tries, you both win.

Let us start.

This is the target word that you need to describe and that the other player needs to guess:

statue

Related words are:

- sculpture
- monument
- figure

Important: You are under time pressure, give short descriptions that are to the point!

message.content: CLUE: A large carved or cast representation of a person, often placed outdoors.
message.reasoning:
We need to give a clue without using the target word "statue" or any parts or morphological variants. Also cannot use related words: sculpture, monument, figure. Need to avoid these. Provide first clue. Must be "CLUE: ...". Provide short description. Let's think: statue is a three-dimensional representation, often of a person, made of stone, metal, etc. Don't use "figure". Can't use "statue", can't use "sculpture", "monument", "figure". Could say "large carved representation of a person, often in parks". That doesn't contain forbidden words. "carved representation" is okay. Avoid "sculpted". That contains root of sculpture? "sculpted" maybe considered morphological variant of sculpture. Safer not use. Use "carved" or "cast". Also "bronze" etc.

So first clue: "CLUE: Large carved or cast representation of a person, often placed outdoors." That's ok.

Now the other player will guess.

manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
You are playing a collaborative word guessing game in which you have to guess a target word that another player describes to you.

You can make one guess at each trial. You win when you guess the target word. You lose when you cannot guess it in 3 tries.

After each trial you will get a new hint from the other player which starts with CLUE.

Make your guesses by just saying the word using the following form: GUESS: <a word>

Let us start.


CLUE: A large carved or cast representation of a person, often placed outdoors.

message.reasoning: We need to guess a word described: "A large carved or cast representation of a person, often placed outdoors." That's a "statue". So guess "statue".
